flags: Concentration: SPY is 30% of the book

plan: CUT SOXL first: trim SOXL (currently 6%) to 3% of book — a single bad month already showed -14.52% and 3x leverage amplifies every semiconductor down-move; place a hard stop at the current session low and do not add back until SMH stabilizes above its own entry zone.
TRIM SPAL to zero or 1%: at -13.72% on a 2x leveraged space name with only 2% weight it contributes noise and tail-risk disproportionate to its size; proceeds go to cash, not a new position.

ADD PANW as a new position, 4% of book: enter 351.34–351.69, hard stop 350.62 (risk ~0.3% of book at 4% size), targets 358.09 / 359.29 / 360.67 — scanner rates it ACCUMULATE 9/12, highest conviction on the list, and it diversifies you away from pure semiconductor risk into cybersecurity/AI software.

WATCH ARM for a 3% of book add only if price holds 336.80 on a pullback: stop 336.18 (risk ~0.1% of book at 3% size), targets 346.15 / 356.29 — R 9.46 is the best reward-to-risk on the list, but ARM is volatile so size is deliberately small; do NOT chase above 337.13.

HOLD SPY, QQQ, QQQI and CRWD with no change: all four are either positive on the month or flagged clear by the exit scanner — CRWD at +7.4% is your best performer and earns its 12% weight; tighten mental stops on QQQ and SPY to roughly -3% from current price to protect the broad-index core that anchors the book.

KEEP a minimum 5% cash buffer after any new adds: with true leverage at 1.14x on margin, cash is your circuit-breaker — it lets you meet a margin call without force-selling winners, and a 2-sigma month is already modeled at -17.91% of equity which exceeds your stop-loss buffer of 9.53%.
read: A 2.5%/month target (~30% annualized) is at the outer edge of what this book's risk profile can sustain consistently — it is not impossible in strong trending months (CRWD's +7.4% shows the upside exists), but the -14.52% on SOXL and -13.72% on SPAL this month illustrate how two small leveraged positions can erase several months of gains before the rest of the book moves.
